title: Business Email Compromise - Wire Fraud Attempt
description: Response playbook for a Business Email Compromise (BEC) where a finance employee receives a payment-redirection request appearing to come from an executive or a known vendor. Triggered when accounts payable flags an unusual change-of-bank-details request or a near-miss / completed fraudulent wire is reported.
author: Cognis Digital

steps.name: Maintain an out-of-band payment-verification policy
steps.description: Ensure accounts payable has a documented callback procedure (using a phone number on file, not one from the email) for any change to vendor banking details or any wire above a threshold.

steps.name: Pre-stage bank and registrar contacts
steps.description: Keep current contacts for the corporate bank's fraud desk and the registrar/host for likely look-alike domains so a recall or takedown can start within the recall window.

steps.name: Triage the suspicious payment request
steps.description: Examine the requesting message for a look-alike or display-name-spoofed sender, an altered reply-to, and pressure language demanding urgency or secrecy.
steps.detection: Mail-gateway logs for the sender domain age and SPF/DKIM/DMARC results; flag external senders whose display name matches an internal executive.

steps.name: Check for mailbox takeover of the real account
steps.description: Determine whether the legitimate executive or vendor mailbox was accessed by the actor rather than merely spoofed.
steps.detection: Identity-provider sign-in logs for impossible-travel or new-ASN logons, plus inbox rules that auto-delete or forward replies to hide the conversation.

steps.name: Halt and attempt recall of the wire
steps.response: If a payment was sent, immediately call the bank fraud desk to request a recall/SWIFT recall and file the kill-chain timeline; freeze any further scheduled payments to the new account.

steps.name: Lock down a compromised mailbox
steps.response: If the real mailbox was taken over, revoke sessions and refresh tokens, reset the password, and remove any hidden forwarding or delete rules the actor created.

steps.name: Remove actor footholds and block infrastructure
steps.response: Block the look-alike domain and sender at the gateway, purge all campaign copies tenant-wide, and revoke any OAuth grants the actor added.

steps.name: Restore normal finance operations under heightened review
steps.response: Resume payments only after re-verifying vendor banking details out-of-band; apply temporary dual-approval on all wires for the affected vendor for a defined period.

steps.name: Report, reconcile, and tune controls
steps.description: File the IC3/law-enforcement report if funds left, reconcile the loss, add the indicators to blocklists, and reinforce the callback policy with the finance team.
